t_ch: 还好没出大事，您下次要小心了。
t_en: Luckily nothing serious happened. You should be careful next time.

t_ch: 我没遇到过。
t_en: Nope. Never met it.

t_ch: 那可真是幸运。
t_en: Then you're lucky.

t_ch: 忆域迷因就像野兽，有些温顺亲人，有些野性暴虐。
t_en: A Memory Zone Meme is like a wild beast. Some are gentle and kindred, while some are barbaric and tyrannical.

t_ch: 遇到迷因的时候，保持情绪的平静有助于延缓迷因的行动，然后就找准时机迅速把自己强制唤醒。要是让某些可怕的迷因缠上了，那可没完没了了。
t_en: When encountering a meme, maintaining your composure is the key to slowing its actions, and then it's just a matter of seizing the right moment to forcibly jolt yourself awake. There's no way out of its clutches if certain terrifying memes get too familiar with you.

t_ch: 下次碰到类似情况，您可以试试这样做。
t_en: Next time you're in a similar situation, you can try doing this.

t_ch: 听说有人私自利用钟表小子的形象为自己牟利，简直太可恶了！
t_en: I heard that someone is privately exploiting the image of Clockie for their own gain. This is simply abhorrent!

t_ch: 钟表小子是梦境世界共同的精神财富，不是某些人的个人财产。
t_en: Clockie is a shared cultural asset of all Dreamscapes. It's not a personal property.

t_ch: 听说最近有人用它的形象推销自己的产品，甚至在未经原作者许可的情况下扭曲钟表小子的设定，太可恶了！
t_en: I heard that someone has been using his image to promote their products lately, even modifying the original settings of Clockie without his creator's permission! This is abhorrent!

t_ch: 更可气的是，每次我去找他们理论，他们总要搬出什么版权之类的东西糊弄我。
t_en: What's even more annoying is that every time I go to them to argue about it, they'll always bring out copyright-related jargon to throw me off.

t_ch: 我听不懂他们颠来倒去的法律术语，我只知道钟表小子不是他们手里的牟利工具！
t_en: I couldn't grasp the twisted legalese they were using, but one thing was clear: Clockie isn't a tool they should use for personal profit!

t_ch: 唉，我还能见到钟表小子博物馆建成的那一天吗？
t_en: Alas, will I ever see the day when the Clockie Museum is built?

t_ch: 自从我成年以来，我便一直在为钟表小子博物馆的建议而奔走。
t_en: Ever since I came of age, I've been actively advocating for the Clockie Museum proposal.

t_ch: 我找过不少于五十位投资人，他们都以种种理由拒绝了我的提议，嘲弄我的想法。
t_en: I've approached at least fifty investors. All of them rejected my offer and mocked my idea for various reasons.

t_ch: 钟表小子相关的物件已经在我的房间里堆积成山了，也许它们终有一日会变作具有珍贵历史价值的文物…但我还能看到那一天吗？有谁能记得我做的努力吗？
t_en: Items related to Clockie have been piling up in my room, and maybe one day, they will become artifacts of significant value... but will I live to see that day? Will anyone remember my efforts?

t_ch: 钟表小子会记得。
t_en: Clockie will remember.

t_ch: 唉，我其实一直很清楚钟表小子是个虚拟形象…也许我是时候该从年少时的幻梦中走出来了。
t_en: *sigh* I'm aware that Clockie is merely a fictional figure... Perhaps it's time for me to let go of the fantastical dreams of my youth.

t_ch: 我会记得。
t_en: I will remember.

t_ch: 感谢你的好意，话说回来，其实没人记得也不会怎么样…世上又有几个人的努力能被记住呢？
t_en: Thank you for your kindness. Speaking of which, it doesn't matter if no one remembers... How many people in the world are remembered for their efforts anyway?

t_ch: 努力过就好了。
t_en: As long as you did your best.

t_ch: 是啊，连钟表小子都没有彻底打倒过它的敌人……
t_en: You're right. Even Clockie has never completely defeated his enemies before...

t_ch: 呸呸呸这些丧气话让您见笑了，您把我说的话忘了吧——钟表小子绝不言败，我也是！
t_en: Blah, what am I doing saying all these depressing words! How embarrassing! Just forget what I said — Clockie never admits defeat, and neither do I!

t_ch: 咳咳…最近流梦礁可真热闹，匹诺康尼上一次聚集这么多无名客，还是古老的囚笼时代呢……
t_en: Ahem... The Dreamflux Reef is really swarming with visitors these days. The last time Penacony saw so many Nameless gathered here was during the old prison days...

t_ch: 年轻人，你应该也是无名客吧？
t_en: Youngster, I guess you're a Nameless too, right?

t_ch: 你让我想起了刚到匹诺康尼的自己…那应该是将近十个琥珀纪之前的事了，我那时也是和你一样意气风发的年轻人，希望能代表「烛墨学派」记录匹诺康尼的历史。
t_en: You reminded me of myself when I first arrived in Penacony... That would have been nearly ten Amber Eras ago, and I was a spirited youngster like you, hoping to record the Penacony history on behalf of the Candelagraphos.

t_ch: 你活了这么久吗？
t_en: Have you lived for such a long time?

t_ch: 是啊，真是好一段漫长的时光，长到历史都已经失真……
t_en: Yeah, it has been a really long time, so long that the history has become unreal...

t_ch: 教教养生秘诀？
t_en: Keen to share your secret to good health?

t_ch: 呵呵，我靠的可不是养生，而是要付出更多代价的方式……
t_en: Hehe, it's not about good health, but rather, how to pay higher prices...

t_ch: 丰饶……
t_en: Abundance...

t_ch: 年轻人，那是属于肉体的赐福，而我早就已经是一个幽灵了……
t_en: Youngster, that is a blessing that belongs to the flesh, and I've long been a ghost...

t_ch: 为了能记录匹诺康尼的未来，我在寿命的终点选择了放弃肉体，成为了一种…只能在梦中存留的迷因。
t_en: To document the future of Penacony, I chose to relinquish my physical form at the end of my lifespan, transforming into a sort of... meme that can only stay within dreams.

t_ch: 作为致力于记录的学者，只是付出这种代价就可以见证更多历史，实在是太划算了。
t_en: As a scholar dedicated to documentation, paying this price to witness more great histories is worth it.

t_ch: 但我写下的史书却被放在匹诺康尼大图书馆的最底层，被小说、漫画、同谐颂歌和致富秘诀掩盖…但在杜撰的繁荣外，我们还能在这片原始的梦里铭刻真实。
t_en: Unfortunately, the history books I've authored now rest at the bottom of the Penacony Grand Library, overshadowed by novels, manga, odes to Harmony, and guides on how to get rich quick... However, amidst the facade of prosperity, we can still etch the truth in this primordial dream.

t_ch: 啊…梦是现实的延伸，记忆的枝桠。时间的光辉从天空洒下，树叶的缝隙间映射出历史的剪影。可那影子太过斑斓，让人看不清面孔，以至于任人打扮。
t_en: Oh... dreams are extensions of reality and branches of memories. As the light of time pours down from the sky, the silhouettes of history are cast upon the gaps in the leaves. Yet the shadows are too patchy to reveal its true face, allowing everyone and anyone to dress it in any way they wish.

t_ch: 记得那时，无名客们就站在我面前。他们说，要把这里变成全宇宙最耀眼的地方。
t_en: As I recalled, the Nameless were standing before my eyes. They said they wanted to make this the brightest place in the universe.

t_ch: 他们做到了，可他们到哪儿去了呢？变成了哈努兄弟、折纸小鸟、镜子公主和左轮队长，除此便什么也不剩下。
t_en: And they did it, but where did they go? They became Brother Hanu, Origami Bird, Mirror Princess, Captain Revolver, and nothing else.

t_ch: 历史会记住他们。
t_en: History will remember them.

t_ch: 未必，想想那些为匹诺康尼献出生命的人，还有几人知晓他们的名字？
t_en: Not necessarily. Think of those who sacrificed their lives for Penacony. How many still know their names?

t_ch: 这是一个没有历史的世界，遗忘是纵情娱乐的终点。
t_en: This is a world without history, and oblivion is indulgence's ultimate end.

t_ch: 我会记得他们的。
t_en: I will remember them.

t_ch: 谢谢你，年轻的无名客，如果「钟表匠」他们知道了，一定会很高兴的。
t_en: Thank you, young Nameless. If the Watchmaker and the others knew about it, they would surely rejoice.

t_ch: 「钟表匠」也是太天真了…即使是动画这种晦涩的形式，即使被记住的不是他自身，也仍希望能够把过去的故事传递到后人手上。
t_en: The Watchmaker was too naive... Even though it was through the vague form of animation, and he might not be remembered by anyone, he still passionately desired to pass on the tales of yore to future generations.

t_ch: 他的工作完成了，但我的事业还在继续。
t_en: He's completed his job, but my career is still ongoing.

t_ch: 而我之后还有后人，后人亦有后人，如此往复，总有些故事能在梦中长存。
t_en: And there will be people after me, then more people will come after they have passed, and so forth. There will always be some stories that will live on in the dreams.

t_ch: 有时间聊聊吗？
t_en: Do you have time for a chat?

t_ch: 如果你对历史有兴趣的话，我可以为你讲上一天一夜。
t_en: If you're interested in history, I can talk with you all day and night.

t_ch: 最近在做什么？
t_en: What are you up to recently?

t_ch: 我吗？还在整理几本童书的读者来信——别用露出那么惊讶的表情。
t_en: Me? I'm still sorting through letters from readers of several children's books — don't look so surprised.

t_ch: 最初我也不赞同这种做法，用幼稚的故事来替代严肃的历史记录，有悖我们学派的原则。可「钟表匠」用他的动画说服了我。
t_en: At first, I didn't agree with this approach either. Replacing important historical records with childish stories goes against the principles of our school. But the Watchmaker's animation convinced me.

t_ch: 况且，我也不是完全做了妥协。他们曾经的对话，那些被我记录在册的故事，我把它们处理后掰碎了一点点地塞进作品里。
t_en: Besides, I didn't completely compromise. The conversation they had and the stories I documented in the book were processed and broken into digestible chunks before being entered into the work.

t_ch: 刚开始的时候，编辑还会为这事儿来找我麻烦，让我不要在故事里加入奇怪的元素和乱码。
t_en: At the start, the editors came at me, telling me not to include strange elements and garbled codes in the story.

t_ch: 可渐渐他们发现，这些内容不仅没有降低销量，反倒激发了读者的解密和背景研究的热情，也就不再多说什么。
t_en: But gradually they realized not only the content did not reduce the sales, it inspired readers to solve the codes and conduct background research. And so, they stopped commenting about it.

t_ch: 和「钟表匠」一样，我绕过家族的监管，我也成功把历史传达了出去。
t_en: Like the Watchmaker, I bypassed The Family's supervision and successfully released the true histories.

t_ch: 以前在做什么？
t_en: What were you doing before?

t_ch: 听说过烛墨学派吗？「秉烛而行，照亮文墨」，不让历史被时间埋葬。而我就是学派的一个记录员。
t_en: Have you heard of the Candelagraphos? "Illuminate each step with candlelight and bring life to the written word" to ensure that time will not bury the past. I am an archiver in the Candelagraphos.

t_ch: 记录正在发生的历史，这正是我来匹诺康尼的目的。不过，记录员也不止我一个，但活到今天的，就只有我了。
t_en: Documenting the history as it is happening is my purpose of coming to Penacony. Of course, I'm not the only archiver, but I'm the only surviving one.

t_ch: 刚脱离公司控制的监狱世界，自然不太安稳，我就这么跟着「钟表匠」他们。一边走，一边记录，有时也帮忙做些书记工作。
t_en: As a prison world that broke free from the IPC's grasp, it is only natural that this place ended up becoming unstable. Hence, I followed the Watchmaker and the others. As I wandered, I meticulously recorded my observations and occasionally assisted with clerical tasks.

t_ch: 他们一会儿勾肩搭背，一会儿又因为晚饭吃什么吵起来，经常吵得我写不进东西。
t_en: At times, they'd wrap their arms around each other's shoulders, only to later bicker over dinner choices. More frequently, I found it impossible to write while assailed by constant commotion.

t_ch: 这时候，我就会威胁他们，谁要是再吵，我就在历史里记一笔，说他是个笨蛋。
t_en: At times like this, I'd threaten them and tell them that whoever was next to argue would be listed as an idiot in the historical annals.

t_ch: 当然，我只是在骗他们，学派不允许篡改历史。可他们不知道啊，就一个个老老实实地坐下来，在我面前装出一副无辜的样子。
t_en: Of course, I was only bluffing. The Candelagraphos doesn't allow us to modify history. But how would they know? And so, they obediently sat down and acted innocent in front of me.

t_ch: 关于流梦礁。
t_en: About the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 这地方不赖吧？既没有家族甜言蜜语的蛊惑，也没有四处巡逻的猎犬。
t_en: Isn't this a great place? Neither The Family's bewitching sweet talk nor the patrolling hounds are around.

t_ch: 就和生命一样，比起被豢养后逐渐失去可塑性的匹诺康尼，这样的梦域反而能够承载更丰富的变化。
t_en: Just like life, such a dreamland can hold richer permutations than Penacony, which had gradually lost its plasticity after being cultured.

t_ch: 它足够原始，在它身上，你能同时看到过去和未来的影子。
t_en: The dreamland is primitive enough for you to see both its past and its future.

t_ch: 看到在监狱的废墟上搭建起新家园的囚犯，看到在梦境的荒漠里飞驰的逐梦客，看到黑暗中不断摸索前路的人们。
t_en: You can see prisoners building new homes on the ruins of their prisons, Dreamchasers who speed through the desert of their Dreamscapes, and people who keep groping their way forward in the darkness.

t_ch: 不过，对常年耽溺在金色美梦里的那些人而言，这里或许确如他们所说，是一场「恐怖的噩梦」吧。
t_en: However, for those who have been drowning in the golden dream for years, this place is nothing but a "horrible nightmare" to them.

t_ch: 先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: Good chatting with you.

t_ch: 再见。
t_en: Goodbye.

t_ch: 再见，愿群星铭记列车的旅途，遗忘永远追不上你们的脚步。
t_en: Farewell. May the stars remember the journey of the Express, and oblivion never match your pace.

t_ch: 历史的美丽之处正在于，它只有寥寥的信息，却能为我们展现出无限的世界。
t_en: The allure of history lies in its sparse collection of facts, which can nonetheless unveil a boundless world of possibilities.

t_ch: 再精确的历史，经历了时间和立场的磨砺，都会变得模糊。采撷其中的真实，也是我们的工作之一。
t_en: However meticulously documented, history will inevitably succumb to the erosion of time and variances of perspective. Thus, sifting through its layers to uncover the truth is an essential aspect of our job.

t_ch: 以我去过的一颗小行星为例，数个琥珀纪以来，那里的居民一直流传着关于「长尾黑面鲎」的传说。
t_en: Taking one of the asteroids I've visited as an example. For several Amber Eras, the inhabitants there have been passing down a legend about the "long-tailed, black-faced horseshoe crabs."

t_ch: 这种生活在冰川深处的巨型节肢动物，以部落的孩童为食。每到长夜降临，人们都会把孩子藏进地窖里，直到第九颗星星升起为止。
t_en: These giant arthropods live deep in the glaciers and feed on the children of the tribes. When the long night falls, people hide their children in cellars until the ninth star rises.

t_ch: 实际上，冰川并不允许巨型动物的生存，所谓的怪物，不过是远古时期冰川另一端敌对部落的图腾。
t_en: Yet the truth remains that the glaciers pose an insurmountable obstacle to the megafauna's survival. These so-called monsters are merely symbolic totems of the rival tribe situated on the other side of the glaciers from time immemorial.

t_ch: 每到长夜，他们就会潜入村庄抢走幼儿。但这个部落对数字九有着未知崇拜，所以行动只会持续到第九颗星星升起。
t_en: Every long night, the rival tribe will sneak into the villages to snatch the young children. But for some unknown reason, this tribe worships the number nine, so the action will only last until the ninth star rises.

t_ch: 而居民们一直以来恐惧的那只巨鲎，早在很久以前，就和自己融合成一个部落了。
t_en: And as for the giant horseshoe crabs that the inhabitants have always feared, they had long ago fused themselves into the tribe.

t_ch: 历史的悲怆之处正在于，无论有多么瑰丽，你能把握的也只有来者。
t_en: The pathos of history lies in no matter how magnificent it is, all we can grasp is what lies ahead.

t_ch: 你见过匹诺康尼现实的星空吗？许多年前，战火还未停息的时候，它们就挂在那儿。我们坐在一片废墟里望着它们，它们也望着我们。
t_en: Have you seen the real stars of Penacony firsthand? Ages ago, before the war ceased, they adorned the sky above us. We sat in the ruins and gazed upward at them as they looked down upon us.

t_ch: 时间流转，匹诺康尼从监狱变成充斥噩梦的荒漠，又从荒漠变成盛会之星。当年一起看星星的人，还能剩下几个呢。
t_en: As time marched on, Penacony transformed from a prison into a desert haunted by nightmares and from a desert into a Planet of Festivities. How many of those who once gazed at the stars together remain in Penacony?

t_ch: 可我只能看着，因为历史不可改变。我没法治好哈努努的伤，没法从忆质黑洞里救回拉扎莉娜，更没法把那只飞蛾与铁尔南带离虫群的包围。
t_en: But I could only watch, for history can never be changed. I couldn't heal Hanunue's wounds, rescue Razalina from the Memoria Singularity, or even save that moth and Tiernan from the Swarm's encirclement.

t_ch: 但你知道吗？我仍记得他们的面孔，记得他们讲的笑话与夜晚的吵闹，可就只是记得而已。
t_en: Do you know? I can still remember their faces, the jokes they told, and their playful voices at night, but that's all I can do.

t_ch: 因为这些都只是历史，它们早已过去，留给我的只有这片深邃的长梦。
t_en: Because it's all history. They are bygone days, leaving me with only this deep and long dream.

t_ch: 这种小吃是以前一位无名客的最爱，但我实在欣赏不来…可每次总忍不住买一点。
t_en: In the past, this snack was one of the Nameless' favorite, but I just couldn't bring myself to appreciate the taste... Yet I can't help but to buy it every time I see it.

t_ch: 要不要试试？或许你会喜欢这种味道。
t_en: Do you want to give it a try? Perhaps you will like it.

t_ch: 你见过匹诺康尼现实的星空吗？许多年前，我们还在战火里的时候，它们就挂在那儿。我们坐在一片废墟里望着它们，它们也望着我们。
t_en: Have you seen the real stars of Penacony firsthand? Ages ago, while we were still at war, they adorned the sky above us. We sat in the ruins and gazed upward at them as they looked down upon us.

t_ch: 时间流转，匹诺康尼从监狱变成荒漠，又从荒漠变成盛会之星。当年看星星的人，还能剩下几个人呢。
t_en: As time marched on, Penacony transformed from a prison into a desert and from a desert into a Planet of Festivities. How many of those who once gazed at the stars together remain in Penacony?

t_ch: 可我只能看着，因为历史不可改变。我没法治好哈努努的伤，没法从忆质黑洞里救回拉扎莉娜，更没法把铁尔南带离虫群的包围圈。
t_en: But I could only watch, for history can never be changed. I couldn't heal Hanunue's wounds, rescue Razalina from the Memoria Singularity, or even save Tiernan from the Swarm's encirclement.

t_ch: 但你知道吗？我仍记得他们的面孔，记得他们讲的笑话与夜晚的吵闹，可就只是记得而已。
t_en: Do you know? I can still remember their faces, the jokes they told, and their playful voices at night, but that's all I can do.

t_ch: 因为这些都是历史，它们早已过去，留给我的只有这片深邃的长梦。
t_en: Because it's all history. They are bygone days, leaving me with only this deep and long dream.

t_ch: 历史的愤慨之处在于，纵使它无法更改，也总会有人尝试去扭曲。
t_en: The infuriating part of history lies in its immutable nature, yet there are always relentless attempts to distort its course.

t_ch: 看看家族现在所做的，简直是在玷污匹诺康尼的过去！
t_en: Look at what The Family is doing now. They are just tarnishing Penacony's past!

t_ch: 淡化历史、漠视历法、令人沉沦美梦…好像这样做，所有的过去就能一笔勾销，悄悄让不利于自己的记载烟消云散，史书只留下家族的赞美诗。
t_en: Dismissing history, disregarding calendars, and drowning everyone in dreams... It's as though through these acts, they seek to erase the past, silently eradicating the unfavorable truths and leaving the history books echoing only the hymns of The Family.

t_ch: 那些追求自由的囚犯，不畏险阻的逐梦客，甚至他们自己手下默默牺牲的成员，都成为如今五大家系的注脚——
t_en: The freedom-seeking prisoners, the odd-defying Dreamchasers, and even the members of their crew who were quietly sacrificed have now become the footnotes of the five major lineages...

t_ch: 不惜磨灭了这么多人的存在，堵上了这么多张嘴巴，只为给家族镀上一副救世主的金身，也不怕吃撑了！
t_en: Going to great lengths to eliminate the existence of countless individuals and silence myriad voices, all to adorn The Family with the illustrious title of savior. Are they not wary of being suffocated by their greed!?

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的今天绝不是梦主或者任何一个人创造的，它的辉煌应当属于所有的匹诺康尼人。
t_en: Today's Penacony is in no way created by the Dreammaster or any one person. Its splendor should belong to all Penaconians.

t_ch: 历史的喜悦之处在于，经历百般苦难，故事仍能传承下去，仿佛延续了往者的生命。
t_en: The joy of history lies in its resilience. Despite countless ordeals, the stories persist as if carrying forward the lives of the dead.

t_ch: 我还没来匹诺康尼的时候，参加过一个古代遗迹的挖掘。遗憾的是，那个文明因为资源缺乏，最终也没能走出自己的星球。
t_en: Before arriving in Penacony, I participated in an excavation of ancient ruins. Regrettably, the local civilization failed to depart the bounds of its planet due to a lack of resources.

t_ch: 早早预料到这一事实的人们，在文明死亡的最后，将所有的故事都刻到了芯片和石头上，用尽全力扔进太空。
t_en: The people who anticipated this fact early carved all their stories onto chips and stones at the passing of their civilization and cast these histories into space with all they had.

t_ch: 直到不知道多少年以后，我们来到刻满了故事的行星环。在最大的一块石头上，他们对我说「陌生人，感谢你的到来」。
t_en: It wasn't until many years later that we arrived at the ring system carved with stories, and on the largest stone, were the engraved words: "Thank you for coming, stranger."

t_ch: 我们一个个地记录并转译下那些故事，故事又流转到宇宙的各个角落，在我们身上，失落的文明获得了第二次进入太空的机会。
t_en: Methodically, we recorded and transcribed the stories, disseminating them to all corners of the universe. Through our endeavors, the lost civilization was afforded a second opportunity to travel the stars.

t_ch: 而你们来到这里，带走匹诺康尼的故事，对我们来说，或许也是一种重生吧。
t_en: And now you are here to take away the tales of Penacony. To us, this is perhaps a kind of rebirth.

t_ch: 这份薄礼还希望你收下，就当是陪我这糟老头子聊天的报偿了。
t_en: Here's a small gift from me. Thanks for chatting with an old geezer like me.

t_ch: 我还没来匹诺康尼的时候，参加过一个古代遗迹的挖掘。遗憾的是，那个文明因为资源缺乏，最终也没能走出自己的星球。
t_en: Before arriving in Penacony, I participated in an excavation of ancient ruins. Regrettably, the local civilization failed to depart the bounds of its planet due to a lack of resources.

t_ch: 早早预料到这一事实的人们，在文明死亡的最后，将所有的故事都刻到了芯片上，用尽全力扔进太空。
t_en: The people who anticipated this fact early carved all the stories onto chips at the death of civilization and threw them into space with all their might.

t_ch: 直到不知道多少年以后，我们来到了刻满了故事的行星环上。我看见，他们对我说「陌生人，感谢你的到来」。
t_en: It wasn't until many years later that we arrived at the ring system carved with stories were the engraved words: "Thank you for coming, stranger" conveyed.

t_ch: 我们一个个地记录并转译下那些故事，故事又流转到宇宙的各个角落，在我们身上，失落的文明获得了第二次进入太空的机会。
t_en: Methodically, we recorded and transcribed the stories, disseminating them to all corners of the universe. Through our endeavors, the lost civilization was afforded a second opportunity to travel the stars.

t_ch: 而你们来到这里，带走匹诺康尼的故事，对我们来说或许也是一种重生吧。
t_en: And now you are here to take away the tales of Penacony. To us, this is perhaps a kind of rebirth.

t_ch: 欢迎光临…哎呀，这位客人也是新来的吧？城寨里最近突然多了许多生面孔，我都快记不过来了。
t_en: Welcome... Oooh, don't tell me we've got another new guest visiting today? All these new faces in town, I can't keep track of them all.

t_ch: 听说很多人都是因为意外从美梦那边过来的，你瞧着应该也差不多……
t_en: I heard lots of people are coming over from the Dreamscapes because of accidents. You look to be the same...

t_ch: 哎呀…不好意思，年纪大了就是容易自顾自唠叨起来，我还是先给你简单介绍一下吧。
t_en: Oh. I'm sorry. It's far too easy to natter on about yourself once you get to my age. Let me give you a brief rundown of what we do.

t_ch: 本店是流梦城寨唯一的乐坊，店里的员工都是像我这样的自动乐器。
t_en: We're the only band in all of the Dreamflux Fortress. All our staff here are talking automated instruments, just like me.

t_ch: 我们的主营业务同样是「自动乐器」，客人只需花费少许苜蓿币，就能带心仪的乐器回家，让它为你演奏。当然，本人是非卖品。
t_en: Our main trade is also in automated instruments. For just a few Alfalfa credits, customers can take home the instrument of their choice and have it play for you at home. Of course, I personally am not for sale.

t_ch: 此外，本店还售卖由我们自己录制的黑胶唱片…如果你觉得无聊了、或是心情不太好，都可以来这里转转。
t_en: We also sell our own vinyl records here... Come here and flip through some if you're ever bored or feeling down in the dumps.

t_ch: ——毕竟，本店的宗旨就是「用音乐为大家带来欢乐」。
t_en: ...I mean after all, our store motto here is "Bring joy to the world through music."

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 当然可以，我很乐意为你提供一切力所能及的帮助。
t_en: Sure can. I'd be more than happy to help you out however I can.

t_ch: 讲讲你的乐坊吧。
t_en: Tell me a little about your band.

t_ch: 乐坊啊…这家乐坊在这座城寨刚刚建立起来的时候就已经存在了。
t_en: Our band... it's been around since this city was first founded.

t_ch: 不过那时候店里只有我一个，我时不时为人们演奏、偶尔录制些唱片，为拓荒者们单调的生活增添些许调剂。
t_en: But back then, I was the only one in the store. I'd often perform for people, and sometimes I'd cut a few records — always adding a little spice to shake up the boring lives of the settlers.

t_ch: 后来…渐渐有其它自动乐器来到这里，其中大部分都有些坎坷波折的经历，而我收留了它们。
t_en: But then, later... other automated instruments gradually came along, most of them with some kind of rough, storied past — and I took them all in.

t_ch: 渐渐地，这里变得热闹起来：我们为人们演奏，用赚取的佣金进行日常的养护…每一天都充实而忙碌。大家的音符中充满欢乐，人们也常因此而获得快乐。
t_en: Gradually, things started to grow more lively here: We'd play for people and put the money we earned toward everyday maintenance... every day was a full and busy one. Everybody's music was brimming with joy in every note, and people got to feel a lot of joy as they listened.

t_ch: 好了，大概就是这样啦…你还有什么想了解的吗？
t_en: Well, that's about all there is... Anything else you'd like to know?

t_ch: 讲讲自动乐器们吧。
t_en: Tell me more about automated instruments.

t_ch: 我的店员们，它们有着不同的来历、迥异的经历，但唯独有一样是共通的——大家都是优秀的乐手。
t_en: My staff all come from different places with different backgrounds, but they also all have one thing in common: Everyone is an outstanding musician.

t_ch: 咆哮先生歌喉奔放粗犷，呐喊出的乐音震人心弦，就同它的外貌那般狂野不羁——但你要照顾好他那几根总是断掉的弦；
t_en: Mr. Growl's voice is rough and bold, and his bellows are every bit as wild as his appearance — just take care to avoid his constantly-breaking strings.

t_ch: 圆笛男爵嗓音嘹亮、音域宽广，它是位高贵的绅士，擅长典雅的乐曲——但他总是耽于自赏，妄想吹奏自己。你如果买走了他，得好好教会他什么叫做谦卑。
t_en: Baron Oboe has a sonorous singing voice and a broad range. He's a classy gentleman who's good with graceful tunes — but he's always making fun for himself and playing whatever music he likes. If you buy him, make sure you teach him a little bit about humility, first.

t_ch: 其它店员们也都有着自己独特的演奏风格与技巧，如果你感兴趣的话，不妨亲自看看它们。
t_en: The other staff all have their own unique playing style and tricks. If you're interested, why don't you go and take a look for yourself?

t_ch: 先聊到这吧。
t_en: That's enough chit-chat for now.

t_ch: 哎呀，不知不觉竟然聊了这么久……
t_en: *Oooh* I didn't realize that we'd been chatting for so long...

t_ch: 我想看看商品。
t_en: I'd like to see the products.

t_ch: 没问题，来挑些你喜欢的吧。
t_en: No problem. Pick out whatever you like.

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 当你希望从音乐中汲取欢乐的时候，欢迎再来这里转转。
t_en: Come back and take a look around next time you feel like you need the joy of music.

t_ch: 哎呀，你又来啦…欢迎光临，有什么需要吗？
t_en: *Oooh* You're back... Welcome back. Can I help you with anything?

t_ch: 你是想要租赁乐器表演，还是想要来张唱片？
t_en: Would you like to rent an instrument, or buy a record?

t_ch: 又或者，是来找我聊聊天呢……
t_en: Or perhaps you've come to have a little chat with me...

t_ch: …你愿意听我讲讲过去的故事吗？
t_en: ...Would you like to hear a little story about my past?

t_ch: 我可是把老提琴了——在我诞生之初，匹诺康尼还是座监狱呢。
t_en: I'm actually an old double bass — back when I was born, Penacony was still a prison.

t_ch: 那时的梦境蛮荒而原始，那时的人们豪放粗犷、无拘无束。我和我的伙伴、我的太阳——阿斯娜，就相识于彼时此地。
t_en: Back then, the Dreamscapes were wild and primitive. The people were bold and savage, too — completely unhinged. It was then and there that I first met my partner, the sun around whom my everything orbits: Asna.

t_ch: 那时我与她常常奏响欢快的乐曲，囚犯们就围在我们身边，看着、听着，他们眼里有光。曲调高昂时他们纵情欢呼、合着节拍起舞……
t_en: We used to play cheerful music together, and the prisoners would gather around to watch and listen, their eyes lighting up. When the melody ramped up, they would cheer heartily, dancing to the rhythm of the beat...

t_ch: 我的琴声与她的歌声回荡在原初梦境的天空里，回荡在璀璨的漫天星光中。那段日子…或许是我这漫长一生中最快乐的时光。
t_en: My strings would reverberate with her voice throughout the sky of that Primordial Dreamscape, echoing in the brilliant starlight. Those days... may just be the happiest days I've ever had in all my years.

t_ch: 后来，那只野狼和乘着火车的人解放了监狱，阿斯娜成了他们口中的「鸢尾花之母」，我也成了博物馆里的一件展品，再也没有见到她。
t_en: Later, after that wolf and the passengers of that train liberated the prison, Asna became what they called "The Mother of Iris" and I became an exhibit in a museum — doomed to never meet again.

t_ch: 如今，我又被一个小偷带回到了这里。我看着这座城寨如何抽芽、如何生发，我录了那么多唱片，都是我们一起合奏过的曲子。
t_en: But then, I was brought back here by some thief. I saw how this city had sprouted and took shape, and I recorded all these vinyl records of the songs we'd once played together.

t_ch: 但那些曲子却不再鲜活生动。它们缺了灵魂，就像梦里少了她的影子……
t_en: But those songs, they no longer felt alive. They lacked a soul, just like I lacked her shadow inside my dreams...

t_ch: 诶呀…抱歉，唠叨了这么久…像我这样上了年纪的老琴，总忍不住找人聊聊…多聊聊，才不容易忘掉那些弦音……
t_en: *sigh*... Sorry for nattering on so long... an old double bass like me can never resist chatting with people... because chatting makes it harder to forget the melody of those strings...

t_ch: 希望那孩子能快些好起来……
t_en: I hope that kid gets better soon...

t_ch: 哎呀，是你来啦？抱歉，我现在正苦恼着呢…介意听我稍稍抱怨一会儿吗？
t_en: Oh. It's you. Sorry. I'm working on something tricky right at the moment... would you mind if I vented my spleen a little?

t_ch: 事情是这样的：最近乐坊来了位新成员，小号先生。那孩子有点内向，性格乖巧、很讨人喜欢…但问题就出在它太过「讨人喜欢」了。
t_en: Here's the thing: We've added a new member to the band recently, Mr. Trumpet, who's a little introverted, kind of cute, and very likable... But the problem is, he's too "likable."

t_ch: 我曾对你说过吧，乐坊的大家都有些坎坷的过往…比如手风琴小姐曾因梦境的侵蚀产生异变、长笛女士曾被合作多年的伙伴抛弃、圆号先生曾因人类孩童的恶作剧遭受严重的损伤……
t_en: I told you before that most of the band came from previously rough lives... like Miss Accordion, who was mutated by a corroded Dreamscape, Miss Flute, who was abandoned by her partner for years, or Mr. French Horn, who was grievously wounded by some humans playing a childish prank...

t_ch: 哎呀，别担心，这些都不是什么秘密，而且大家也早就走出了曾经的阴影。那么现在说回小号先生……
t_en: *Oooh* Don't worry. These aren't secrets at all and everyone has left their dark pasts well behind them. So then, getting back to Mr. Trumpet...

t_ch: 它的音色因忆质侵蚀变得有些浑浊，但这并非问题所在。它很…自卑。即便大家都在努力安慰它、照料它，但它似乎依旧缺乏安全感。
t_en: His timbre has been corroded by memoria, but that's not the real problem. He's... got an inferiority complex. He seems to be really insecure even though everyone constantly reassures and looks after him.

t_ch: 而直到前些天我才发现…原来它竟一直在私下里偷偷练习，日以继夜地演奏、演奏到活塞都出现了轻微的磨损。而这一切只因为…它觉得「只有能够完美地演出，才不会再次被抛弃」。
t_en: And I didn't realize until just the other day that... he had been rehearsing in secrecy the whole time, day and night, until his pistons started to get worn down from overuse. And he was doing this because... he had this thought that he could only avoid abandonment through perfect musical performances.

t_ch: …或许我有些反应过度了，但我确实感到恼火——当然，这情绪并非针对小号先生，而是我自己。
t_en: ...Maybe I overreacted, but I felt really angry at the time — not at Mr. Trumpet, of course, but rather directed at myself.

t_ch: 是否是因为我疏于照顾，才没能给它足够的安全感？是不是我并没有尽到应尽的责任……
t_en: Did I not give it enough sense of security because I failed to care for him enough? Was it because I didn't do everything I could for him...

t_ch: 我理应…也必须能尽己所能，为我的「家人」做些什么…对吧？
t_en: I should... no, I MUST do everything I can for my "kinsmen"... right?

t_ch: …或许我有些反应过度了，但我确实感到恼火——当然，这情绪并非针对小号先生，而是我自己。
t_en: ...Maybe I overreacted, but I felt really angry at the time — not at Mr. Trumpet, of course, but rather directed at myself.

t_ch: 是否是因为我疏于照顾，才没能给它足够的安全感？是不是我并没有尽到应尽的责任……
t_en: Did I not give it enough sense of security because I failed to care for him enough? Was it because I didn't do everything I could for him...

t_ch: 我理应…也必须能尽己所能，为我的「家人」做些什么…对吧？
t_en: I should... no, I MUST do everything I can for my "kinsmen"... right?

t_ch: 我很享受现在的生活……
t_en: I love my life at the moment...

t_ch: 哎呀，刚刚有个常来光顾的年轻人，问我要不要和他一起回去美梦去。
t_en: Oh. A young customer who's one of our regulars just came by asking if I wanted to go back to the sweeter Dreamscapes with him.

t_ch: 可我去美梦做什么呢？我的心已经老了，再弹不出热情欢快的曲子啦。所以我问他：「难道是想让我回到博物馆的橱窗里吗？」
t_en: But what am I going to do there? My heart's already too old to play those cheery songs once more. So I asked him: "Don't tell me you're trying to take me back to that display cabinet in the museum?"

t_ch: 哈哈…那个小家伙似乎挺失望，但对于现在的我来说，美梦太喧闹，这里却正好。
t_en: Haha... that poor kid seemed pretty disappointed, but at this age I find the sweet dreams too noisy. Things are perfect where I am now.

t_ch: 这里有我的乐坊、我的「家人」们。我可以隔着薄薄的橱窗玻璃，看它们演出、看它们用音符为人们带来欢乐。
t_en: Here, I have my band and my "kinsmen." Through just a razor-thin piece of glass, I can observe them performing — plus all the joy that their melodies bring people.

t_ch: 那是种真实无比、纯粹无比的欢乐…我曾在囚犯们的眼睛里见过它，我曾在阿斯娜的指尖感受到它，而如今它又出现在我的面前——
t_en: That's a kind of pure, true joy... I'd seen it in those prisoners' eyes. I'd felt it at Asna's fingertips, and now it had arrived once more before me...

t_ch: 「用音乐为大家带来欢乐」…看吧伙伴，这个约定，我最终还是好好遵守了啊。
t_en: "Bring joy to the world through music"... Look at me now, Asna. This promise of ours: I've kept my word until the very end.

t_ch: 对于现在的我来说，美梦太喧闹，这里却正好。
t_en: At this age I find the sweet dreams too noisy. Things are perfect where I am now.

t_ch: 这里有我的乐坊、我的「家人」们。我可以隔着薄薄的橱窗玻璃，看它们演出、看它们用音符为人们带来欢乐。
t_en: Here, I have my band and my "kinsmen." Through just a razor-thin piece of glass, I can observe them performing — plus all the joy that their melodies bring people.

t_ch: 那是种真实无比、纯粹无比的欢乐…我曾在囚犯们的眼睛里见过它，我曾在阿斯娜的指尖感受到它，而如今它又出现在我的面前——
t_en: That's a kind of pure, true joy... I'd seen it in those prisoners' eyes. I'd felt it at Asna's fingertips, and now it had arrived once more before me...

t_ch: 「用音乐为大家带来欢乐」…看吧伙伴，这个约定，我最终还是好好遵守了啊。
t_en: "Bring joy to the world through music"... Look at me now, Asna. This promise of ours: I've kept my word until the very end.

t_ch: 亲爱的客人，欢迎光临，想吃点什么？
t_en: My dear guest, welcome! What would you like to eat?

t_ch: 本店是流梦礁的老字号，品种一应俱全。翠丝的面包要说第二，没人敢在流梦礁说第一。
t_en: We're a vintage Dreamflux Reef brand with everything you could possibly need. If someone says Jessie's bread is only second best, then there is no champion bread in the Dreamflux Reef.

t_ch: 介绍下面包吧。
t_en: Tell me more about this bread.

t_ch: 听上去像虚假宣传。
t_en: Sounds like fake news to me.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，您可真幽默。我们小本经营，顾客都是老居民，要是不诚信早就被大家赶跑啦。
t_en: Haha, you're a funny one. We're a small owner-operated business and our customers are all long-time locals. If we weren't honest, we would have gotten driven away years ago.

t_ch: 能形容下有多好吃吗？
t_en: Can you describe for me how delicious it is?

t_ch: 硬要说的话，既不华丽，也不新奇，就像是小时候妈妈做的那种味道——本店在制作过程中，只讲究一个厚道。
t_en: If you insist: It's not overly gorgeous or innovative. Rather, it's just like the flavor mom used to bake when you were a child. During the baking process, all we focus on is sincerity.

t_ch: 本店的面包以及其他的所有食物，所用的原料都来自纯净有机的忆域农田。
t_en: All bread and other foodstuffs in our store use pure organic ingredients sourced from the Memory Zone's farms.

t_ch: 它们生长的土壤不含有有害的情绪，亦不掺杂消极与负面的记忆，所以由它们制作的精神食粮尝起来口味温和醇厚，回味甘甜。
t_en: The soil they grow in is devoid of harmful emotions and negative memories, so the spirit fuel it produces has this mild and mellow taste with a lingering sweet aftertaste.

t_ch: 等您醒来之后，就会发现食用这类产品的好处了。这可比美梦里的快餐健康多了。
t_en: You'll soon realize the benefits of eating this once you wake up. This is far healthier than the fast food in the sweet dream.

t_ch: 您可以待会看看菜单，不急着选~
t_en: Check out the menu whenever you're ready. No rush~

t_ch: 可以聊聊吗？
t_en: Are you up for a chat?

t_ch: 没问题，面包和故事，我这里都有。
t_en: No problem. Bread and stories — we've got the works.

t_ch: 为什么会开这家店？
t_en: Why did you open this kind of store?

t_ch: 想起了已经是很久之前的事情了。从我离开美梦，来到流梦礁后，因为一直很喜欢烹饪，便想到开一家店来维持生计。
t_en: It's something that happened long, long ago. When I first left the Dreamville for the Dreamflux Reef, I wanted to open my own store to make a living and continue my love of cooking.

t_ch: 我尝试了好多新奇的菜品，发现大家还是最喜欢朴实无华、量大管饱的食物，所以就坚持下来一直做了。
t_en: I tried out many an experimental dish but discovered that everyone preferred filling themselves with simple meals in sufficiently large portions. So I kept going and making them all.

t_ch: 现在想想，之前和我同一时间开的那些店，也只有我一家一直开到现在了。唉，沧海桑田啊。
t_en: Now that I think about it, my store's the only one left out of all those stores that opened up around the same time as mine. *sigh* How things change!

t_ch: 平时的生意怎么样？
t_en: How is business normally?

t_ch: 以前流梦礁人多的时候，每天这里都排着长队，等着热腾腾的面包出炉。
t_en: Back when there were more people around the Dreamflux Reef, this place would have a long queue out the door every day waiting for fresh hot bread to come out of the oven.

t_ch: 现在不行喽，人越来越少，也只有老顾客来光顾了。不知道你有没有碰到那个整天笑嘻嘻的维德，他可是每天雷打不动地买三个面包。生意都是靠他们撑着。
t_en: But not now. Fewer and fewer people come in, and we're down to regular customers only. I wonder if you've met Vader who just sits around grinning all day? He never fails to buy three loaves of bread from us, every day. Our very business depends on these people.

t_ch: 不过最近来了不少像你这样的年轻人，但愿未来会变好一些吧。
t_en: But these days we've seen quite a few youngsters like you coming through, so hopefully things will get better in the future.

t_ch: 有遇到什么有趣的客人吗？
t_en: Ever meet any interesting customers?

t_ch: 有趣的客人…那可太多了，您不就是吗？
t_en: Interesting customers, eh... ? Too many of those to count. And don't you count yourself as one?

t_ch: 开个玩笑。若说让人印象最深刻，还是一位从我年轻时就时常光顾这里的老人。一杯咖啡，一块面包，一条老狗，他能待一下午。
t_en: Just kidding. If you're talking about someone who let an impression: There was this old man who came by regularly back when I was young. A cup of coffee, a loaf of bread, and this old dog: Enough for him to stay the entire afternoon.

t_ch: 他最会用钟表小子来讲故事，有时啊能把整个屋子的人都逗笑，可惜…他已经不会再来了。
t_en: He'd tell the best stories using Clockie as the medium, and sometimes he'd have the entire room in stitches laughing along. But unfortunately... he won't be coming back again.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all the questions I have.

t_ch: 我想看看吃的。
t_en: I'd like to see the food.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临，翠丝的店永远为你敞开~
t_en: See you again next time — Jessie's store will always be open to your business~

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 欢迎下次光临，翠丝的店永远为你敞开~
t_en: See you again next time — Jessie's store will always be open to your business~

t_ch: 烘焙的香味，就是幸福的味道！
t_en: Fresh baking: That's the smell of happiness!

t_ch: 在流梦礁最困难的时候，大家食不果腹，精神低落。
t_en: Back when the Dreamflux Reef was facing dire times, nobody had enough food to eat and spirits were very low.

t_ch: 每次小麦粉到货，我们坐在店里，将一大块面包切成小块大家轮流分享，连咖啡也是你喝一小口，再传给我喝一小口。
t_en: Every time a flour delivery arrived, we'd all sit in the store and chop a big loaf into smaller pieces of bread for everyone to take turns sharing. Even the coffee was shared between people, sip by sip.

t_ch: 最后大家都笑了，这过的叫什么日子嘛！
t_en: Everyone ended up hunched over laughing. What kind of day do you call that!?

t_ch: 到现在，磨粉、揉面、发酵、烘烤…这样的工序我已经重复了不知道多少遍，但每次闻到烘烤带来的热腾腾、暖洋洋的香味时，我就会感到由衷的幸福。
t_en: All the grinding, kneading, fermenting, and baking... I've repeated this process countless times, but every time I smell that steamy warm scent of something baking, I'll get this feeling of sincere happiness.

t_ch: 大家团结在一起度过难关，有这么好的一群人，流梦礁未来也一定不会差——这话我跟科玫不知说了多少次，她还年轻，她太相信那些数字，然而重要的是我们的心。
t_en: With such a talented group all working together to overcome hardships, the future of the Dreamflux Reef is looking great — I don't know how many times I've said this to Kaimee because she is still young and still trusts the data too much, but what's most important is our spirit.

t_ch: 最近来了不少年轻人，也许我也应该开发一些新口味。
t_en: A lot of youngsters have been coming by the store these days. Maybe I should branch out into some new flavors?

t_ch: 虽然我们也算老店了，但固步自封只能被时代淘汰。
t_en: We might be a trusted old brand, but if we remain stuck in our old ways, we'll soon be obsolete.

t_ch: 难得碰到外面的年轻人，你们现在喜欢什么样的食物？
t_en: Such a rare chance to chat with a youngster from outside. What kind of foods do you like to eat now?

t_ch: 苏打豆汁。
t_en: Mung Bean Soda.

t_ch: 仙舟的特色饮料吗？我好像在家族的图书馆里看到过。可惜不知道配方呢。
t_en: Is that the Xianzhou specialty drink? I think I saw it once in The Family's library. What a pity I don't know the recipe for it.

t_ch: 橡木蛋糕卷。
t_en: Oak Cake Rolls.

t_ch: 独特的口味…听说橡木家系的人能面不改色地吞下一整块呢。
t_en: What a unique taste... They say that members from the Oak Family can swallow a whole piece of the cake without batting an eyelid.

t_ch: 太多了我写下来吧。
t_en: Hang on. Let me get a pen.

t_ch: 我不喜欢食物。
t_en: I don't like food.

t_ch: 她用奇怪的眼神看着你，然后仿佛明白了什么……
t_en: She shoots you a strange look seeming to comprehend something...

t_ch: 你…难不成是智械？
t_en: So you... are you an Intellitron?

t_ch: 我不是智械。
t_en: I am not an Intellitron.

t_ch: 不用不好意思，我们不歧视智械的。
t_en: Don't be embarrassed about it. We don't discriminate here against Intellitrons.

t_ch: 哔哔哔，我是智械。
t_en: Bibibi! I. Am. Intellitron.

t_ch: 现在的智械已经这么先进了吗…你们智械应该会喜欢能源类的食物吧，下次我会试着研究电池和机油套餐的。
t_en: Modern day Intellitrons are so advanced... You should like energy for food, right? I'll try to look into a battery and machine oil combo for next time.

t_ch: 感谢！一会我整理一下新菜单，敬请期待！
t_en: Thanks! I'm taking care of the new menu. Stay tuned!

t_ch: 竟然有人在我们的精神食粮里以次充好，甚至想要用忆质食品全面取代现制菜品！
t_en: There are actually people out there selling counterfeit copies at a mark-up, and even some who want to replace all food with memoria fuel!

t_ch: 现实的食品滋养我们的肉体，梦境中的食物充实着我们的精神。从某种意义上说，精神食粮的重要性一点也不逊色于现实中的食物。
t_en: Real food nourishes our bodies, while Dreamscape food enriches our spirit. In a way, spirit fuel is no less important than real food.

t_ch: 最近我进的货质量越来越差，制造这些原料的厂商还和我抱怨，大家都以次充好，他们要是不同流合污根本赚不到钱。
t_en: I've been getting worse and worse stock lately, and the manufacturers who make the raw materials complain about people selling counterfeit copies at a mark-up. Unless they themselves stoop as low, they can't make any money.

t_ch: 更有甚者，开了一家忆质食品公司——把关于食物的记忆复制一下就能制造食物，热一热就能吃…他们试图用这种方便又劣质的东西垄断梦境的食品行业。
t_en: What's more, they even started up a memoria fuel company — copying food-related memoria to manufacture food that becomes good to eat as soon as you heat it up... They are trying to corner the Dreamscape's food market with their convenient but inferior product.

t_ch: 这种东西吃多了会把自己的味觉搞坏的！但那些不知情的年轻人只能买他们的廉价菜饭裹腹，这样吃下去他们的精神状态怎么会好？
t_en: Eating this stuff will break your taste buds! Those unsuspecting youngsters gorge themselves on that cheap stuff. How are their spirits going to fare if that keeps on?

t_ch: 唉，人心不古，世风日下啊！
t_en: *sigh* Public decency nowadays. The world is going to the dogs!

t_ch: 唉，真是想念他……
t_en: *sigh* I miss him so much...

t_ch: 当我离开家族来到流梦礁之后，曾有一段迷茫无助的时间——我开了一家咖啡店，但大家视我为间谍和敌人，生意寥寥无几，快到了倒闭的边缘。
t_en: When I first left The Family and came to the Dreamflux Reef, I had this period of helpless confusion — I opened up a cafe but was viewed as a spy and the enemy. Business dwindled, and we were quickly on the brink of bankruptcy.

t_ch: 这时，一位老人开始每天光顾我的小店，他点了一杯咖啡，自己画着钟表小子的分镜，一条黑犬趴在他的脚边打盹，日复一日……
t_en: Just then, this old guy started visiting our cafe every day. He'd order a coffee and doodle pictures of Clockie while a dog snoozed at his feet. Day in, day out...

t_ch: 从那以后起，大家逐渐放下了对我的怀疑，店里的生意也一天比一天好。
t_en: From that moment forth, everyone gradually let go of their doubts about me, and business at the cafe got better and better.

t_ch: 有一天我鼓足勇气向他道谢，他微笑着向我点头，送了一幅钟表小子的画给我，并称赞了我的手艺。
t_en: One day, I worked up the courage to approach him, but he just laughed and nodded at me. He gave me a picture of Clockie, and praised me for my baking skills.

t_ch: 后来我才知道，那位平易近人的老人就是让家族头疼的搅局者，匹诺康尼的最为传奇的大亨。
t_en: I later learned that kind old man had been behind The Family's troubles and was Penacony's most legendary tycoon.

t_ch: 他现在已经不在了，但我的店即便没有他也天天有顾客造访，这是他留给我的礼物…相信每一位流梦礁的居民，都应该收到过类似的礼物吧……
t_en: He is no longer with us anymore, but even without him, customers still visit our store. That's a gift that he left behind for me... I trust all the residents of Dreamflux Reef have all received the same gift...

t_ch: 你和这只蓝色的小鸟面面相觑。它似乎没有要开口的意思。
t_en: You and the blue bird stare at each other, but it doesn't look like it's going to speak anytime soon.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: …同一个把戏玩两次就没意思了。
t_en: ...It's pointless to play the same trick twice.

t_ch: 又见面了。
t_en: Hello again.

t_ch: 啧，来得太快了，我还没休息够呢。
t_en: Tsk, here so soon? I'm still tired.

t_ch: 啾？
t_en: Tweet?

t_ch: …找鸟找傻了？
t_en: ...Did you go silly from hunting for birds?

t_ch: 你和这只蓝色的小鸟面面相觑。它似乎没有要开口的意思。
t_en: You and the blue bird stare at each other, but it doesn't look like it's going to speak anytime soon.

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: …哎，又一个没看过《钟表小子》的土老帽。
t_en: ...Bah, another bumpkin who's never seen Clockie before.

t_ch: 显而易见，我是只折纸小鸟，你也可以叫我湛蓝爵士。
t_en: Obviously, I'm an origami bird. You can call me Dr. Blues.

t_ch: 啾？
t_en: Tweet?

t_ch: …找鸟找傻了？
t_en: ...Did you go silly from hunting for birds?

t_ch: 算了，既然来了，那领个任务再走吧。
t_en: Never mind, since you're here, take this mission before leaving.

t_ch: 我想想…按照「故事情节」来说，这一章我该扮演「善良的折纸小鸟」，而你是「帮我寻回伙伴的好心客人」。
t_en: Let's see... According to the plot, I should be playing the kind origami bird in this chapter, and you're the kind-hearted guest who helps me find my companions.

t_ch: ——但扮鸟太累了，我拒绝。光是修这座「清醒村」就够我忙的了。
t_en: ...But playing a bird is tiring, so I'm refusing this role. I'm busy enough fixing up Rouseville.

t_ch: 总之，只要你找回足够数量的小鸟，我就会给你对应的奖励。
t_en: Anyway, I'll give you your just rewards as long as you get enough birds back.

t_ch: 听懂了吗？懂了就出发吧。
t_en: You got that? You should get going if you have.

t_ch: 咦？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 看来你已经找到一半小鸟了。
t_en: Looks like you've already found half of the birds.

t_ch: 这是来自折纸小鸟的「谢礼」。拿好，慢走不送。
t_en: This is a gift from the origami birds. Take it, and don't let me keep you.

t_ch: 咦？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 看来你已经找到所有小鸟了。
t_en: Looks like you found all the birds.

t_ch: 太好了，可以提前杀青了——记得拿好「谢礼」，慢走不送。
t_en: Excellent, we can wrap up early. Don't forget to take this gift, and don't let me keep you.

t_ch: …又怎么了？我现在忙的很，有话快说。
t_en: ...What is it now? I'm super busy. Well? Spit it out.

t_ch: 关于你自己…
t_en: About yourself...

t_ch: 折纸小鸟「湛蓝爵士」，或者筑梦师「奥尔拉」。只要别给我添乱，想怎么叫都行。
t_en: Origami Bird Dr. Blues, or Dreamweaver Orla. You can call me whatever you want as long as you don't cause trouble for me.

t_ch: …算了，这个名字从你嘴里吐出来总感觉怪怪的…你还是叫我「湛蓝爵士」吧。
t_en: ...Never mind, this name sounds weird when you're the one saying it... Better just address me as Dr. Blues.

t_ch: 给我讲讲折纸小鸟的故事…
t_en: Tell me the story of the origami birds...

t_ch: 折纸小鸟的故事？自己看《钟表小子》动画去，讲得比我清楚多了。
t_en: The origami bird story? Go watch the Clockie series yourself — they explain it much clearer than I would.

t_ch: 关于清醒村…
t_en: About Rouseville...

t_ch: 清醒村是《钟表小子》动画第二部的故事舞台：「穿过美梦小镇的影子，钟表小子来到了一座与众不同的神奇村落。这里聚集着许多被美梦驱赶的村民：爱撒谎的猎犬、疯疯癫癫的猫头鹰，还有不会啾的折纸小鸟……」
t_en: Rouseville is where the story is set in the second Clockie series: "Through Dreamville's shadow, Clockie arrives at a wondrous village that is quite distinct. Many residents cast out by Dreamjoy have gathered here: A Bloodhound who lies compulsively, an owl that has gone quite mad, and an origami bird that doesn't tweet..."

t_ch: ——如何，听起来好玩吗？我瞎编的。
t_en: ...How about it? Sounds fun eh? I wrote it off the top of my head.

t_ch: 所谓清醒村，其实就是{RUBY_B#流梦礁}这里{RUBY_E#}。一处收留那些不为美梦所容的可怜虫的「庇护所」罢了。
t_en: This so-called Rouseville is actually {RUBY_B#Dreamflux Reef}this place{RUBY_E#}. Nothing more than a safe haven for those poor souls who were abandoned by Dreamjoy.

t_ch: 关于折纸小鸟…
t_en: Tell me about the origami birds...

t_ch: 你见过忆域迷因吗？那是一种因人的情感与欲望而诞生的梦境生命体——或者说一种类似于生命体的「现象」。你见到的折纸小鸟也是差不多的东西。
t_en: Have you seen a Memory Zone Meme before? That is a Dreamscape lifeform born out of people's emotions and desires, or simply put, it's a "phenomenon" akin to a lifeform. The origami birds you've seen are no different from that.

t_ch: 广泛的群众认知，足够充分的执念，再加上一点点来自于神秘的「谎言」…捏个折纸小鸟也不是什么难事。
t_en: With widespread audience awareness, ample determination, and a hint of Enigmata's lies... making an origami bird isn't such a hard task.

t_ch: 只要注意别让它们跑到梦境之外就好——哦，但我不算，我属于特例。
t_en: Just make sure they don't get outside the Dreamscape — oh, except for me I mean. I'm the exception.

t_ch: 感谢那条猎犬，看来他的「谎言」放在现实里也能生效。
t_en: I'll have to thank that Hound. Looks like his "lie" also works in the real world.

t_ch: 关于猫头鹰老师…
t_en: About Professor Owl...

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师是美梦小镇的设计者，是钟表小子的老朋友，是折纸小鸟们的老师——当然，这只是动画故事里的设定。
t_en: Professor Owl is the one who designed Dreamville and Clockie's old friend. He's the teacher of the origami birds. Of course, that's just how it was set up in the cartoon's story.

t_ch: 在过去的匹诺康尼，人们都叫他「格拉克斯教授」，曾经是来自博识学会的扎兹卡学者，后来是隐夜鸫家系的首领。
t_en: In the Penacony of the past, people called him Professor Glaux. He was once a Zatzka scholar from the Intelligentsia Guild, and later became the leader of the Nightingale Family.

t_ch: 不知道「钟表匠」用了什么办法，才让他愿意抛下星空生态学派的首席教授职称，跑到一无所有的梦境里开荒……
t_en: I don't know how the Watchmaker got him to willingly abandon his post as Head Professor of the Astral Ecology School to come to this forsaken wasteland in the Dreamscape...

t_ch: 他是个伟大的筑梦师，也是个伟大的老师…只可惜现在除了那条吵闹的大街，没什么地方能看到他的名字。
t_en: He is both a great Dreamweaver and a teacher... It's a pity that his name has been buried in obscurity — you won't find it mentioned anywhere.

t_ch: 我需要一些提示。
t_en: I need a few hints.

t_ch: 提示？当然可以，我知道所有小鸟的位置。
t_en: A hint? Certainly, I know where all the birds are.

t_ch: 一枚硬币换一条线索——不许讨价还价。
t_en: A coin for a clue, and no haggling.

t_ch: （拿出一枚艾迪恩代币）
t_en: (Take out an Aideen Token)

t_ch: 小鸟矜持地叼过你拿出的代币，然后…吃掉了它！
t_en: The bird gingerly picks up the coin you give it, and... swallows it!

t_ch: 让我看看，还有哪个倒霉蛋没被抓到呢……
t_en: Let's see which unlucky fella hasn't been caught yet...

t_ch: 嗯…有个到处捣乱的小鸟老爱在别人的作品上恶作剧，<color=#dbc291ff>入口那边的海报上</color>看看吧，它估计又在那干坏事呢。
t_en: Hmm... There's a mischievous bird that loves playing pranks on others. Take a look at <color=#dbc291ff>the poster by the entrance</color>. It could be up to no good there.

t_ch: 嗯…有只小鸟最近老是郁郁寡欢，或许现在也在<color=#dbc291ff>垃圾堆那边</color>唉声叹气吧。
t_en: Hmm... There's a bird that's been in low spirits recently. Maybe it's still sullenly pottering about by the <color=#dbc291ff>garbage pile</color>.

t_ch: 嗯…折纸小鸟都喜欢恶作剧呢，最近有人说<color=#dbc291ff>入口那边的灯泡</color>经常闪烁，或许就是某个小鸟的手笔。

For some reason, it's always fixated on adding unnecessary details to other people's art pieces... Could it be because birds can't hold a paintbrush?

t_en: Hmm... There's a bird nearby somewhere... right in front of me by <color=#dbc291ff>that chair</color>. Do you see it?

t_en: Hmm... There's a bird that's been crowing about buying balloons recently. I shooed it away towards the business street. Go find it <color=#dbc291ff>near the shop that sells balloons</color>. It's probably strolling around in its vicinity.

t_ch: 所以我们搭建了这个锻炼身体的赛场，只要大家变得比谁都厉害，坏蛋就不敢欺负我们了啾。
t_en: So we built this racetrack to train our bodies. As long as everyone became stronger than everything else, baddies wouldn't dare bully us again, tweet.

t_ch: 在赛场上得到冠军的镇民就能成为美梦小镇的「大明星」！哈努兄弟、钟表小子、音符小姐、仓鼠球骑士、木头老爷、猫头鹰老师，还有我们这几只鸟都是大明星！
t_en: Residents who won on the racetrack would become Dreamville's stars! Brother Hanu, Clockie, Miss Note, Hamster Ball Knight, Old Man Wood, Professor Owl, and us birds here, we're all stars!

t_ch: 关于小镇居民…
t_en: About the townspeople...

t_ch: 美梦小镇的狠角色可不少，比如汽水先生，看起来成天呆头呆脑的，真遇到困难还得靠他帮忙啾。
t_en: There are plenty of menacing characters here in Dreamville, like Mr. Soda. He looks like he just muddles around all day, but we rely on him when it really counts, tweet.

t_ch: 还有钟表小子，别看他两条腿短短的，喝下苏乐达之后，跑起来可没几只鸟能追上他啾。
t_en: And Clockie too. Don't be fooled by his short legs. Once he gulps down SoulGlad, few birds can keep up with his speed, tweet.

t_ch: 关于猫头鹰老师…
t_en: About Professor Owl...

t_ch: 嘿呀！你也知道那位伟大的猫头鹰老师？全美梦小镇我最敬佩的就是它老人家啾。
t_en: Oho! You also know our great Professor Owl? He's the one I look up to the most in Dreamville, tweet.

t_ch: 别看老师总待在树上，搭建美梦小镇的时候，森林里藏着不少坏蛋，老师和钟表小子把它们都解决了，是硬派鸟中的硬派鸟啾。
t_en: He may look like he just perches in a tree all day, but when we were building Dreamville, there were so many baddies lurking in the forest. The Professor and Clockie took care of them all. They're the most hardcore among the real hardcore birds, tweet.

t_ch: 我毕生最大的梦想，就是成为一只和老师一样厉害的鸟啾。
t_en: My lifelong dream is to become an amazing bird like the Professor, tweet.

t_ch: 我需要一些提示。
t_en: I need a few hints.

t_ch: 唔…虽然小家伙们逃跑时一哄而散，但就凭它们的速度，还逃不出我的眼睛……
t_en: Hah... Those little fellas may be speedy when they're fleeing, but slowpokes like them won't even be able to escape my eyes...

t_ch: 只是最近记性有点差…你手头有神奇的的「魔法硬币」吗？有它我就能想起来了啾！
t_en: It's just that my memory's been hazy recently... Do you have any of those wondrous magic coins? I'll be able to remember with them, tweet!

t_ch: （拿出一枚艾迪恩代币）
t_en: (Take out an Aideen Token)

t_ch: 嘿呀！是魔法硬币！
t_en: Hi-yah! It's a magic coin!

t_ch: 小鸟粗暴地叼过你拿出的代币，然后…吃掉了它！
t_en: The bird snatches the coin from you viciously, and then... gobbles it up!

t_ch: 哼哼，记忆涌上来了啾……
t_en: Hehe, my memories are returning, tweet...

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个害羞的小家伙，最近在偷偷练习唱歌，<color=#dbc291ff>大厅的那些音响</color>，应该很适合它啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: There's a shy fella that's been learning to sing in secret. The <color=#dbc291ff>lobby with audio equipment</color> should be perfect for it to practice, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个热心肠的小家伙特别爱到处帮忙，说不定又混进<color=#dbc291ff>入口的迎宾队列里</color>，给大家引路去了啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: There's a passionate one that loves helping out. It could have stolen into the <color=#dbc291ff>welcome line at the entrance</color> again, trying to give directions to people, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，苏乐达是小鸟们的最爱，在<color=#dbc291ff>汽水瀑布那里</color>，多半能找到贪嘴的小鸟啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: Birds love SoulGlad, so you'll have a high chance of finding a greedy bird by the <color=#dbc291ff>soda waterfall</color>, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，音符小姐让不少小鸟有了新的梦想，有个爱上了舞台的小家伙，现在还在找它的<color=#dbc291ff>星星</color>啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: Miss Note has invoked new dreams among the birds. There's one that's obsessed with stage plays, and is still finding its <color=#dbc291ff>stars</color>, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，<color=#dbc291ff>赛场上时常会出现很精彩的表演</color>，停留在周围的小家伙，一旦入戏太深，就容易忘记回家啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: <color=#dbc291ff>there are always exciting performances at the racetrack</color>. Anyone that stop by will get too engrossed in the show and forget to go home, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个擅长拍摄的小家伙，特别喜欢热血的场面，最近一直沉迷在<color=#dbc291ff>动作派的现场</color>拍戏啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: There's a fella that's great at photography, and loves places that get your blood pumping. It's recently been obsessed with productions at the <color=#dbc291ff>School of Action site</color>, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，最近<color=#dbc291ff>赛场上来了只大猩猩</color>，把所有对手都打得落花流水，那个爱看戏的小家伙绝对不会错过啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: A <color=#dbc291ff>huge gorilla has recently arrived at the racetrack</color> and demolished all its opponents. That fanfare-loving fella will definitely not miss this, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，你见过恐龙吗？据说，那东西也是一种小鸟。<color=#dbc291ff>如果有只恐龙参加比赛</color>，周围肯定少不了观赛的小家伙啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: You've seen a dinosaur before? People say it's also a type of bird. <color=#dbc291ff>If a dinosaur joins the competition</color>, there'd be plenty of birds spectating, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，哈努兄弟和猫头鹰老师是多年的老朋友，就连他的据点外面也有老师的痕迹，<color=#dbc291ff>是小鸟看了就会亲近的雕像啾</color>。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: Brother Hanu and Professor Owl are longtime friends. There are even vestiges of the Professor outside his usual hangout: <color=#dbc291ff>It's a statue that birds are reflexively drawn to, tweet</color>.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，石头老板似乎找到了<color=#dbc291ff>哈努兄弟的据点</color>，有个勇敢的小家伙，已经飞去救他了啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: It seems like Boss Stone has located <color=#dbc291ff>Brother Hanu's usual hangout</color>. A brave little one has already flown there to save him, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，赛场里新建了<color=#dbc291ff>一台非常大的时钟</color>，每次飞过那里，总能听见熟悉的声音啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: <color=#dbc291ff>A giant clock</color> was recently built at the racetrack. You can hear familiar sounds each time you fly by there, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，<color=#dbc291ff>有个傻乎乎的小家伙跑去找谜钟先生玩了，可那里最近有恐龙出没</color>，万一被坏蛋吃掉就糟糕了啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: <color=#dbc291ff>There's a dimwitted one that's gone to look for Mr. Big Ticker to play, but dinosaurs have recently been spotted there</color>. It'd be trouble if it got eaten by a baddie, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个小家伙兴冲冲的对我说，它要和钟表小子一起拍戏，依我看，无非就是藏在<color=#dbc291ff>钟表小子的广告牌</color>里啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: A lil' fella enthusiastically told me that it wanted to shoot a movie with Clockie. I think it's just hiding inside <color=#dbc291ff>Clockie's billboard</color>, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，<color=#dbc291ff>不久前来了个红头发的骑士</color>，有个小家伙被他迷得神魂颠倒，总是躲在一边远远望着他啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: <color=#dbc291ff>A red-haired knight recently stopped by</color>. One of the birds is besotted by him, always admiring the knight from afar, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个贪玩的小家伙，最近又迷上了<color=#dbc291ff>赛道两边的转转机</color>，真希望它别给赛场的工作人员添乱啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: There's a playful fella that's recently been obsessed with the <color=#dbc291ff>spinny machines flanking the racetrack</color>. I hope it doesn't cause trouble for the staff there, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个稳重的小家伙，帮了哈努兄弟不少的忙，你大概可以在<color=#dbc291ff>哈努兄弟的办公室</color>找到它啾。
t_en: Heya! Let's see, there's a reliable little fella that's helped Brother Hanu out plenty. You can probably find it <color=#dbc291ff>in Brother Hanu's office</color>, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个特别喜欢哈努兄弟的小家伙，非要去见他，现在应该还在<color=#dbc291ff>哈努兄弟的休息室</color>等他啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: There's a fella that loves Brother Hanu and insists on visiting him. It's probably still waiting for him at <color=#dbc291ff>Brother Hanu's lounge</color>, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，<color=#dbc291ff>入口的大厅里不是有很多照片吗？</color>小家伙们总在那儿叽叽喳喳地议论参赛的选手，完全没理解比赛的意义啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: <color=#dbc291ff>Aren't there a lot of photos by the entrance lobby?</color> The birds are always hunkered there discussing race contenders — they've completely missed the essence of the competition, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，比赛结束后，很多设备都需要及时维护，负责这些的那个小家伙，应该还在<color=#dbc291ff>赛场的哪个角落</color>干活啾。
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: After a competition ends, a lot of equipment needs to be maintained promptly. That bird in charge of this equipment should still be working <color=#dbc291ff>in some corner of the racetrack</color>.

t_ch: 嘿呀——让我想想，有个小家伙吵着要去看冠军，也不知道它有什么办法，<color=#dbc291ff>总不至于到终点去拦住人家啾。</color>
t_en: Hi-yah!!! Let me recall: There's a little fella that keeps yelling about wanting to see the champion. I don't know what it's up to, <color=#dbc291ff>but I sure hope it's not waiting to accost others at the finish line, tweet.</color>

t_ch: 还是算了吧。
t_en: Never mind.

t_ch: 嘿呀，硬派鸟即使没有帮助也能出色完成任务——啾啾！
t_en: Hi-yah, real hardcore birds are able to complete the task perfectly without help, tweet tweet!

t_ch: 「一枚硬币换一条提示」，这可是小鸟的铁律啾！
t_en: A coin for a clue. This is an ironclad rule among birds, tweet!

t_ch: 无论如何，请先把上一个小家伙带回来再说吧！啾啾！
t_en: Regardless, let's bring back that previous little one first! Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 以后再说吧。
t_en: Catch you next time.

t_ch: 那就快去把小家伙们找回来吧，啾啾！
t_en: Hurry and bring the little ones back, tweet tweet!

t_ch: 啾！小家伙们，我现在就要你们知道逃跑的代价啾——等我训练完它们就来和你比试啾。
t_en: Tweet! Little ones, I will let you all know the price of escaping, tweet. Come back and challenge me when I'm done with their training, tweet.

t_ch: 好快的动作啾！如果小家伙们都有你这样的速度，就再也不会害怕石头老板了啾。
t_en: You move fast, tweet! If only the little fellas could be as speedy as you, we'd have nothing to fear from Boss Stone, tweet.

t_ch: 这是你的报酬，硬派鸟绝不会让朋友白跑一趟啾。
t_en: Here's your reward. Real hardcore birds will never let their friends' efforts be in vain, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀！你居然真把它们都抓回来了，本鸟今天甘拜下风啾。
t_en: Hee-ya! You really caught them all! I concede to your greatness today, tweet.

t_ch: 从现在起，你就是美梦小镇飞得最快的鸟了啾！这些报酬通通都给你啾。
t_en: From now on, you're the fastest bird in the Dreamville, tweet! All these rewards are for you, tweet.

t_ch: 嘿呀！何等硬派的速度，逃跑的小家伙们竟然全给你抓到了啾。
t_en: Hee-ya! You've got some hardcore speed! The escaped little fellas have all been caught by you, tweet.

t_ch: 从现在起，你就是美梦小镇飞得最快的鸟了啾！这些报酬通通都给你啾。
t_en: From now on, you're the fastest bird in the Dreamville, tweet! All these rewards are for you, tweet.

t_ch: 今天破例给小家伙们放假一天——要来和我比试吗？啾啾？
t_en: I made an exception today and gave the little fellas the day off. How about a race with me? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 怎么办？怎么办？急死鸟了啾。
t_en: What do I do? What do I do? Ooh, I'm just about to tear my feathers out, tweet.

t_ch: 人家就打了个盹儿，小鸟们就跑没影了啾。
t_en: I dozed off for a moment, and the birds were nowhere to be seen, tweet.

t_ch: 你这是？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 哎呀～客官您出现得可真及时，是专程来安慰人家的吗？啾啾？
t_en: Ooh, you've shown up just in time. Have you specially come to comfort me? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 您不在的这段时间，人家想您想得羽毛都掉了好几根，翅膀都没以前好看了啾～
t_en: While you weren't around, I missed you so much that even a few of my feathers fell out. My wings look worse for wear too, tweet...

t_ch: 人家现在遇到了好大的困难，客官您一定要帮帮人家啾。
t_en: Now that I'm in big trouble, you must help me out, tweet.

t_ch: 哎呀～是位面生的客官，是专程来见人家的吗？啾啾？
t_en: Oho, a familiar customer. Did you come all this way to see me? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 如果不是在这种时候，人家一定会好好地招待客官，让您毕生难忘的啾。
t_en: If you had come another time, I'd definitely show you a good time that you wouldn't soon forget, tweet.

t_ch: 可惜人家现在还有急事，只能先委屈客官您等等了啾。
t_en: But I've something urgent to attend to. You'll have to make do and wait for me, tweet.

t_ch: 你误会了。
t_en: You misunderstand.

t_ch: 小鸟的眼神里似乎凭空多了一丝委屈，让你不忍心再否定下去。
t_en: The bird's eyes show a sliver of hurt out of nowhere — even you're hard-pressed to deny it.

t_ch: 呜呜，原来客官不是为了人家来的，不过人家真的很需要您的帮助啾。
t_en: Boohoo, so you didn't come here for me. I really do need your help, though, tweet.

t_ch: 我等得起。
t_en: I've got some time.

t_ch: 小鸟冲你露出它水汪汪的大眼睛，仿佛感动得下一刻就会哭出来。
t_en: The bird directs its watery big eyes at you, as if it'd cry in sheer gratitude.

t_ch: 原来客官这么看重人家，等我把小家伙们找回来，一定陪您逛遍美梦小镇啾。
t_en: So you do really care about me. Once I round up those little ones, I'll be sure to accompany you on your tour around Dreamville, tweet.

t_ch: 细说毕生难忘。
t_en: Tell me about this unforgettable experience.

t_ch: 这个嘛，真说起来，和客官您聊上三天三夜也说不完啾。
t_en: Oh, this? Even three days wouldn't be enough if I went into detail, tweet.

t_ch: 绝对不会辜负客官您期待的啾。
t_en: I absolutely won't let you down, tweet.

t_ch: 这里是？
t_en: What is this place?

t_ch: 这里是鸟儿唱歌的地方——飘荡着乐曲的「好梦剧场」啾。
t_en: This is the Dreamgood Theater, where birds sing and melodies glide and mingle, tweet.

t_ch: 有时音符小姐也会来表演，给小鸟们传授唱歌的技巧，所以剧场里有很多慕名而来的观众啾。
t_en: Miss Note sometimes performs here too, where she teaches the birds the art of song. It's also the reason the theater's always packed with audiences from afar, tweet.

t_ch: 我带着孩子们来和音符小姐学唱歌，结果一个不留神，它们就全跑掉了啾。
t_en: I was watching the kids while Miss Note was giving singing lessons, but I lost concentration for just a moment, and they all disappeared, tweet.

t_ch: 那都是些精力充沛的孩子，万一打扰到其他客人，被老师知道了，一定会生气的啾。
t_en: They're all energetic children. If they disturbed other guests, the Professor would be mad if he knew.

t_ch: 客官，您就忍心看着一只可爱的小鸟为此落泪吗？啾啾？
t_en: Honored guest, would you bear to see an adorable bird shed tears because of this? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 你自己去。
t_en: Go do it yourself.

t_ch: 哎呀～如果有小鸟飞回来找人家，不就扑空了吗？啾啾？
t_en: Oh dear, wouldn't they miss me if birds came back looking for me? Tweet tweet?

t_ch: 好吧，我来帮你。
t_en: All right, I'll help you.

t_ch: 我就知道，客官您不会丢下人家不管的，这就是故事里的英雄救美啾。
t_en: I knew it! You wouldn't abandon me in the time of need. You're like my knight in shining armor in the stories, tweet.

t_ch: （听不见）
t_en: (Pretend not to hear)

t_ch: 客官，这里就我们一人一鸟，就算您装作听不见也是没用的啾！
t_en: Honored guest, it's just you and me here. It's pointless even if you pretend not to hear me, tweet!

t_ch: 拜托客官了，如果您在剧场里看到了那些调皮的孩子，请让它们回大树这里，人家会好好奖励您的啾！
t_en: Please, if you see those mischievous kids in the theater, tell them to return to the Great Tree. I'll reward you handsomely, tweet!

t_ch: 啾？
t_en: Tweet?

t_ch: 客官您已经带回了这么多孩子吗？啾～
t_en: Oh wow, you've already brought back so many kids? Tweet!

t_ch: 看来人家也得表示一下呢，要好好珍惜这份礼物啾！
t_en: It appears I also have to express my gratitude. Please treasure this gift, tweet!

t_ch: 啾？
t_en: Tweet?

t_ch: 哎呀，客官您已经把所有的小捣蛋鬼都找到了啾。
t_en: Oh wow, you found all those naughty little children, tweet.

t_ch: 看来人家也得表示一下呢，要好好珍惜这份礼物啾！
t_en: It appears I also have to express my gratitude. Please treasure this gift, tweet!

t_ch: 啾～客官想找人家聊聊天吗？只要您能想到的问题，人家都知无不言啾。
t_en: Tweet! Did you wish to speak to me? I've got all the answers, as long as you've got any questions, tweet.

t_ch: 关于你自己…
t_en: About yourself...

t_ch: 哎呀，客官您居然对人家这么感兴趣吗？小鸟们都叫我「妃色夫人」啾。
t_en: Oh dear, are you really that interested in me? The little birds call me Lady Vermilion, tweet.

t_ch: 作为猫头鹰老师的学生，无论是优雅的蓝调街，还是舒心的大花园，您在小镇上看到的大多数美好的事物，都是人家的手笔啾。
t_en: As Professor Owl's student, whether it's the elegant Blues Street, or the soothing garden, all the beautiful things you see in town are of my design, tweet.

t_ch: 给我讲讲折纸小鸟的故事…
t_en: Tell me the story of the origami birds...

t_ch: 俗话说，了解一只鸟，要从它的过去开始，客官您真是有心了啾。
t_en: As the saying goes: To know a bird, you must start from its past. You're really sweet to do that, tweet.

t_ch: 关于好梦剧场…
t_en: About the Dreamgood Theater...

t_ch: 据说，在猫头鹰老师还没搭建出美梦小镇的时候，好梦剧场就已经在这儿了啾。
t_en: It's even said that the Dreamgood Theater already existed before Professor Owl even established Dreamville, tweet.

t_ch: 石头老板手下的那些坏蛋，总说剧场是它们的祖先建的，人家才不信啾！
t_en: Boss Stone and his minions keep insisting that their ancestors built the theater first. I say poppycock to that, tweet!

t_ch: 这么漂亮的剧场，怎么看都是给小鸟们唱歌的地方啾。
t_en: Any way you look at it, this magnificent theater is a place meant for birds to flock together to sing, tweet.

t_ch: 关于小镇居民…
t_en: About the townspeople...

t_ch: 美梦小镇的大家都是好人、好小鸟，虽然一个个脾气都古古怪怪的啾。
t_en: The folks in Dreamville are good people, good birds. Even if their tempers are eccentric, tweet.

t_ch: 人家最羡慕的就是音符小姐，只要她唱起歌来，小镇里的争吵全都会停下来，在教育小鸟们的事情上，她也帮了不少忙啾。
t_en: I envy Miss Note the most — when she sings, all the conflicts in town go silent. And she's helped out a lot in educating the birds, tweet.

t_ch: 如果人家也有她那么好听的声音，一定能给美梦小镇招揽来更多客人啾。
t_en: If I had a voice as beautiful as hers, I could definitely attract more visitors to Dreamville, tweet.

t_ch: 关于猫头鹰老师…
t_en: About Professor Owl...

t_ch: 如果说美梦小镇里还有比人家更懂得「美」的鸟，那就非猫头鹰老师莫属了啾。
t_en: If there's anyone who knows "beauty" better than anyone in Dreamville, Professor Owl's the one and only expert, tweet.

t_ch: 哪些石头适合用来搭房子，哪种树枝可以和花一起装饰巢穴，猫头鹰老师的作品，永远都能抓住最关键的部分，让小石子变得闪闪发光啾。
t_en: Which stones are good for building houses, and which branches can be used to adorn nests with flowers... Professor Owl's designs perennially capture the essence of beauty, allowing every little pebble to shimmer and shine, tweet.

t_ch: 希望未来有一天，人家也能搭出那么好看的鸟巢啾。
t_en: I hope one day in the future, I can also build a nest that's just as pretty, tweet.

t_ch: 我需要一些提示。
t_en: I need a few hints.

t_ch: 唔，虽然很想帮客官，但人家也想不起来它们去哪里了，啾……
t_en: Oh, I would love to help you, but I can't remember where they went either. Tweet...

t_ch: 客官，如果您有「魔法硬币」就好了，魔法硬币可以唤醒人家的记忆啾！
t_en: Dear guest, it'd be fantastic if you have a "magic coin." Magic coins can reawaken my memories, tweet!

t_ch: （拿出一枚艾迪恩代币）
t_en: (Take out an Aideen Token)

t_ch: 啾！是魔法硬币啾！
t_en: Tweet! A magic coin, tweet!

t_ch: 小鸟温柔地叼过你拿出的代币，然后…吃掉了它！
t_en: The bird gently holds the token you gave it in its mouth, and then — swallows it whole!
